 {"id":"beads_rust-135","title":"Implement br update self-update command","description":"# Self-Update Command Implementation

## Purpose
Enable br to update itself to the latest version with a simple command, using cryptographic verification for security.

## Technical Requirements

### Commands
```bash
br update              # Check and install latest version
br update --check      # Check only, don't install
br update --force      # Force reinstall current version
br update --version X  # Install specific version
```

### Dependencies
```toml
[dependencies]
self_update = { version = "0.39", features = ["rustls"], default-features = false }
```

### Implementation
```rust
use self_update::backends::github;
use self_update::cargo_crate_version;

fn update_self(check_only: bool, force: bool) -> Result<()> {
    let status = github::Update::configure()
        .repo_owner("Dicklesworthstone")
        .repo_name("beads_rust")
        .bin_name("br")
        .show_download_progress(true)
        .current_version(cargo_crate_version\!())
        .build()?
        .update()?;
    
    match status {
        Status::UpToDate(v) => println\!("Already at latest version: {}", v),
        Status::Updated(v) => println\!("Updated to version: {}", v),
    }
    Ok(())
}
```

### Security Features
- SHA256 checksum verification
- Download over HTTPS only (rustls)
- Atomic binary replacement
- Verify before delete old binary

### Output
```
$ br update
Checking for updates...
Current version: 0.1.0
Latest version:  0.2.0

Downloading br v0.2.0...
[████████████████████████████████] 100%

Verifying checksum...
Installing...
✓ Updated br from 0.1.0 to 0.2.0

$ br update --check
Current version: 0.2.0
Latest version:  0.2.0
✓ Already up to date
```

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] `br update` downloads and installs latest
- [ ] `br update --check` only checks
- [ ] Checksum verification works
- [ ] Progress bar during download
- [ ] Atomic replacement (no partial updates)
- [ ] Works on Linux, macOS, Windows

 {"id":"beads_rust-15v","title":"stats Command Implementation","description":"## Overview
Implement the `br stats` command for displaying project health metrics and issue statistics. Provides a quick overview of the issue database state.

## CLI Interface
```
br stats [OPTIONS]

Options:
  --by-type                   Break down by issue type
  --by-assignee               Break down by assignee
  --by-priority               Break down by priority
  --by-label                  Break down by label
  --since <DATE>              Stats since date (for velocity)
  --json                      Output as JSON
  --robot                     Machine-readable output
```

## Implementation Details

### Core Statistics
```rust
struct ProjectStats {
    total_issues: usize,
    open_issues: usize,
    in_progress_issues: usize,
    closed_issues: usize,
    
    blocked_issues: usize,
    ready_issues: usize,
    
    // Velocity (if --since provided)
    created_since: Option<usize>,
    closed_since: Option<usize>,
    
    // Breakdowns
    by_type: Option<HashMap<IssueType, TypeStats>>,
    by_assignee: Option<HashMap<String, AssigneeStats>>,
    by_priority: Option<HashMap<u8, usize>>,
}
```

### SQL Queries
```sql
-- Basic counts
SELECT 
    COUNT(*) as total,
    COUNT(CASE WHEN status = 'open' THEN 1 END) as open,
    COUNT(CASE WHEN status = 'in_progress' THEN 1 END) as in_progress,
    COUNT(CASE WHEN status = 'closed' THEN 1 END) as closed
FROM issues;

-- Ready vs blocked
SELECT 
    COUNT(CASE WHEN id NOT IN (SELECT issue_id FROM blocked_issues) THEN 1 END) as ready,
    COUNT(CASE WHEN id IN (SELECT issue_id FROM blocked_issues) THEN 1 END) as blocked
FROM issues
WHERE status = 'open';

-- By type
SELECT issue_type, status, COUNT(*) as count
FROM issues
GROUP BY issue_type, status;

-- By assignee
SELECT 
    COALESCE(assignee, '(unassigned)') as assignee,
    COUNT(*) as total,
    COUNT(CASE WHEN status = 'open' THEN 1 END) as open,
    COUNT(CASE WHEN status = 'in_progress' THEN 1 END) as in_progress
FROM issues
GROUP BY assignee;
```

## Output Formats

### Human-readable (default)
```
Project Statistics
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════

Total Issues:    156
  Open:          42  (27%)
  In Progress:   8   (5%)
  Closed:        106 (68%)

Ready to Work:   35
Blocked:         7

By Type:
  feature        45 (12 open)
  bug            38 (15 open)
  task           52 (10 open)
  epic           12 (3 open)
  docs           9  (2 open)

By Priority:
  P0 (critical)  3  (2 open)
  P1 (high)      18 (8 open)
  P2 (medium)    89 (22 open)
  P3 (low)       35 (8 open)
  P4 (backlog)   11 (2 open)
```

### JSON
```json
{
  "total": 156,
  "by_status": {
    "open": 42,
    "in_progress": 8,
    "closed": 106
  },
  "ready": 35,
  "blocked": 7,
  "by_type": {
    "feature": { "total": 45, "open": 12 },
    "bug": { "total": 38, "open": 15 }
  }
}
```

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Show total/open/in_progress/closed counts
- [ ] Show ready vs blocked breakdown
- [ ] Optional breakdown by type
- [ ] Optional breakdown by assignee
- [ ] Optional breakdown by priority
- [ ] Optional breakdown by label
- [ ] Velocity stats with --since
- [ ] Human-readable and JSON output

## Dependencies
- Requires SQLite Storage Layer
- Requires blocked_issues cache

## Rationale
Stats provide situational awareness for project health. "How many bugs are open?" "Who has the most work assigned?" "What's our velocity?" These questions should be answerable instantly without custom queries.

 {"id":"beads_rust-1bi","title":"update Command Implementation","description":"# update Command

## Purpose
Update issue fields with strict validation, label operations, claim flow, and correct event/dirty behavior.

## CLI
```
br update <id...> [OPTIONS]
br update [OPTIONS]  # Uses last-touched if no ID
```

## Core Flags

### Field Updates
- `--title <text>`: Update title (1-500 chars).
- `--description <text>`: Update description (alias: `--body`).
- `--design <text>`: Update design notes.
- `--acceptance <text>`: Update acceptance criteria.
- `--notes <text>`: Update additional notes.

### Workflow
- `--status <status>`: Change status (open, in_progress, blocked, deferred, closed).
- `--priority <0-4|P0-P4>`: Change priority.
- `--type <type>`: Change issue type.

### Assignment
- `--assignee <name>`: Assign to user (empty string clears).
- `--owner <name>`: Set owner (empty string clears).
- `--claim`: Atomic claim (assignee=actor + status=in_progress).

### Scheduling
- `--due <date>`: Set due date (empty string clears).
- `--defer <date>`: Set defer-until date (empty string clears).
- `--estimate <minutes>`: Set time estimate.

### Labels
- `--add-label <label>`: Add label(s).
- `--remove-label <label>`: Remove label(s).
- `--set-labels <labels>`: Replace all labels with these.

### Relations
- `--parent <id>`: Reparent to new parent (empty string removes parent).
- `--external-ref <ref>`: Set external reference.

### Session
- `--session <id>`: Set closed_by_session when closing.

## Behavior
1. **ID Resolution**: Accept multiple IDs. If none provided, use last-touched.
2. **Change Detection**: Only apply flags that were explicitly provided.
   - Important: P0 priority must detect flag-changed (not just value).
3. **Claim Flow** (`--claim`):
   - Set assignee to current actor.
   - Set status to `in_progress`.
   - Fails if already assigned to someone else.
4. **Parent Reparenting** (`--parent`):
   - Remove existing parent-child dependency.
   - Add new parent-child dependency to new parent.
   - Empty string removes parent without adding new one.
5. **Label Operations**:
   - `--add-label`: Append to existing labels.
   - `--remove-label`: Remove from existing labels.
   - `--set-labels`: Replace all labels entirely.
6. **Field Clearing**: Empty string (`""`) clears optional fields (due, defer, assignee, owner).
7. **Content Hash**: Recompute content_hash on field changes.
8. **Events**: Emit appropriate events (`field_changed`, `status_changed`, etc.).
9. **Dirty Marking**: Mark issue as dirty for export.

## Output

### JSON
```json
[
  {
    "id": "bd-abc12",
    "title": "Updated title",
    "status": "in_progress",
    "priority": 1,
    "updated_at": "2025-01-15T10:30:00Z"
  }
]
```

### Text Output
```
Updated bd-abc12: Updated title
  status: open → in_progress
  priority: P2 → P1
```

### No Updates
```
No updates specified for bd-abc12
```

## Error Handling
- **IssueNotFound**: ID does not resolve → error.
- **AmbiguousId**: ID resolves to multiple → error with candidates.
- **AlreadyClaimed**: `--claim` on issue assigned to someone else → error.
- **InvalidStatus**: Status not recognized → error with valid values.
- **InvalidPriority**: Priority not in range → error.
- **TitleTooLong**: Title exceeds 500 chars → error.
- **ParentNotFound**: `--parent` ID does not exist → error.
- **CycleDetected**: Reparenting would create cycle → error.

## Logging
```rust
tracing::info!(ids = ?ids, "Updating issues");
tracing::debug!(field = "status", old = %old, new = %new, "Field changed");
tracing::info!(id = %id, assignee = %actor, "Issue claimed");
tracing::debug!(id = %id, old_parent = ?old, new_parent = ?new, "Reparenting issue");
tracing::warn!(id = %id, "No changes applied");
```

## Acceptance Criteria
- Claim behavior matches bd.
- Parent reparenting updates deps correctly.
- JSON output matches bd.
- Multiple IDs handled atomically.
- Field clearing with empty string works.

    br_cmd(&beads_dir)
        .args(["update", &id, "--title", "New title"])
        .assert()
        .success();
    
    let output = br_cmd(&beads_dir)
        .args(["show", &id, "--json"])
        .output()
        .unwrap();
    let json: Value = serde_json::from_slice(&output.stdout).unwrap();
    assert_eq!(json[0]["title"], "New title");

#[test]
fn test_update_reparent() {
    let (_, dir) = init_beads();
    let beads_dir = dir.path().join(".beads");
    
    let parent1 = create_issue(&beads_dir, "Parent 1");
    let parent2 = create_issue(&beads_dir, "Parent 2");
    
    br_cmd(&beads_dir)
        .args(["create", "Child", "--parent", &parent1])
        .assert()
        .success();
    
    let child = get_last_issue_id(&beads_dir);
    
    // Reparent to parent2
    br_cmd(&beads_dir)
        .args(["update", &child, "--parent", &parent2])
        .assert()
        .success();
    
    // Check dependency
    let output = br_cmd(&beads_dir)
        .args(["dep", "list", &child, "--json"])
        .output()
        .unwrap();
    let json: Value = serde_json::from_slice(&output.stdout).unwrap();
    
    // Should have parent-child dep to parent2, not parent1
    let deps = json.as_array().unwrap();
    let has_parent2 = deps.iter().any(|d| 
        d["depends_on_id"].as_str().map(|s| s.contains(&parent2)).unwrap_or(false)
    );
    assert!(has_parent2);
}

    br_cmd(&beads_dir)
        .args(["update", &child, "--parent", ""])
        .assert()
        .success();
    
    // Check no parent-child dep
    let output = br_cmd(&beads_dir)
        .args(["dep", "list", &child, "--json"])
        .output()
        .unwrap();
    let json: Value = serde_json::from_slice(&output.stdout).unwrap();
    let deps = json.as_array().unwrap_or(&vec![]);
    let has_parent_dep = deps.iter().any(|d| 
        d["type"].as_str() == Some("parent-child")
    );
    assert!(!has_parent_dep);
}

    let beads_dir = dir.path().join(".beads");
    
    let id1 = create_issue(&beads_dir, "Issue 1");
    let id2 = create_issue(&beads_dir, "Issue 2");
    let id3 = create_issue(&beads_dir, "Issue 3");
    
    br_cmd(&beads_dir)
        .args(["update", &id1, &id2, &id3, "--status", "in_progress"])
        .assert()
        .success();
    
    // All should be in_progress
    for id in [&id1, &id2, &id3] {
        let output = br_cmd(&beads_dir)
            .args(["show", id, "--json"])

        let json: Value = serde_json::from_slice(&output.stdout).unwrap();
        assert_eq!(json[0]["status"], "in_progress");

    let (_, dir) = init_beads();
    let beads_dir = dir.path().join(".beads");
    
    let blocker = create_issue(&beads_dir, "Blocker");
    let blocked = create_issue(&beads_dir, "Blocked");
    
    br_cmd(&beads_dir)

        .assert()

 {"id":"beads_rust-3aa","title":"Child Counters and Hierarchical IDs","description":"## Overview
Implement child counters for hierarchical issue IDs. This enables epic/sub-issue relationships with readable IDs like `bd-abc.1`, `bd-abc.2`, `bd-abc.1.1`.

## Technical Requirements

### Hierarchical ID Format
```
<prefix>-<hash>.<child1>.<child2>...

Examples:
  bd-abc12       # Root issue
  bd-abc12.1     # First child of bd-abc12
  bd-abc12.2     # Second child of bd-abc12
  bd-abc12.1.1   # First child of bd-abc12.1
```

### Child Counters Table
```sql
CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS child_counters (
    parent_id TEXT PRIMARY KEY,
    next_child INTEGER NOT NULL DEFAULT 1,
    updated_at TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT (datetime("now"))
);
```

### ID Generation with Hierarchy
```rust
impl SqliteStorage {
    /// Get next hierarchical ID for a parent
    pub fn next_child_id(&mut self, parent_id: &str) -> Result<String> {
        self.begin_transaction()?;
        
        // Get or create counter for parent
        let counter = self.conn.query_row(
            "SELECT next_child FROM child_counters WHERE parent_id = ?",
            [parent_id],
            |row| row.get::<_, i64>(0)
        ).unwrap_or(1);
        
        // Increment counter
        self.conn.execute(
            "INSERT INTO child_counters (parent_id, next_child) VALUES (?, ?)
             ON CONFLICT(parent_id) DO UPDATE SET next_child = next_child + 1, updated_at = datetime(\"now\")",
            params![parent_id, counter + 1]
        )?;
        
        self.commit()?;
        
        Ok(format!("{}.{}", parent_id, counter))
    }
    
    /// Allocate a new root ID (no parent)
    pub fn allocate_root_id(&mut self) -> Result<String> {
        let prefix = self.get_prefix()?;
        let hash = generate_adaptive_hash(self)?;
        Ok(format!("{}-{}", prefix, hash))
    }
}
```

### Parent-Child Relationship
```rust
impl Issue {
    /// Extract parent ID from hierarchical ID
    pub fn parent_id(&self) -> Option<&str> {
        // bd-abc12.1 -> Some("bd-abc12")
        // bd-abc12.1.2 -> Some("bd-abc12.1")
        // bd-abc12 -> None
        let last_dot = self.id.rfind(".")?;
        Some(&self.id[..last_dot])
    }
    
    /// Get depth in hierarchy (0 = root)
    pub fn depth(&self) -> usize {
        self.id.matches(".").count()
    }
    
    /// Check if this is a child of another issue
    pub fn is_child_of(&self, potential_parent: &str) -> bool {
        self.id.starts_with(potential_parent) && 
        self.id.len() > potential_parent.len() &&
        self.id.chars().nth(potential_parent.len()) == Some(".")
    }
}
```

### Creating Child Issues
```rust
fn create_child_issue(parent_id: &str, title: &str, storage: &mut SqliteStorage) -> Result<Issue> {
    // Verify parent exists
    let parent = storage.get_issue(parent_id)?
        .ok_or(BeadsError::IssueNotFound(parent_id.into()))?;
    
    // Get next child ID
    let child_id = storage.next_child_id(parent_id)?;
    
    // Create child issue
    let child = Issue {
        id: child_id,
        title: title.into(),
        parent_id: Some(parent_id.into()),
        ..Default::default()
    };
    
    storage.create_issue(&child)?;
    
    // Auto-create waits-for dependency (parent waits for all children)
    storage.add_dependency(&Dependency {
        issue_id: parent_id.into(),
        depends_on_id: child.id.clone(),
        dependency_type: DependencyType::WaitsFor,
    })?;
    
    Ok(child)
}
```

```bash
# Create child issue under epic
br create "Implement login" --parent bd-abc12
# Creates bd-abc12.1

br create "Add OAuth" --parent bd-abc12
# Creates bd-abc12.2

br create "Test OAuth flow" --parent bd-abc12.2
# Creates bd-abc12.2.1
```

## Purpose
Maintain the most recently touched issue ID for commands that omit IDs (update/close/show).

## Storage
- File: `.beads/last-touched`
- Permissions: `0600`
- Content: single ID + newline

## Behavior
- `SetLastTouchedID(id)`: best-effort (ignore errors).
- `GetLastTouchedID()`: returns empty on missing/invalid file.
- `ClearLastTouched()`: best-effort delete.

## Which Commands Set It
- `create`: set to new issue ID.
- `update`: set to **first updated** ID.
- `show`: set to **first shown** ID (or routed ID).
- `close`: **does not** update last-touched.

## Acceptance Criteria
- File permissions correct (0600).
- Missing file is non-fatal.

- Set/get/clear behaviors.

 {"id":"beads_rust-dc4","title":"Implement --robot-triage flag for ranked actionable items","description":"# --robot-triage Flag Implementation

## Purpose
Provide AI coding agents with a ranked list of actionable items, including scores, reasons, and unblock information to enable intelligent work selection.

## Technical Requirements

### Output Format
```json
{
  "recommendations": [
    {
      "id": "beads_rust-xyz",
      "title": "Implement search command",
      "priority": 1,
      "score": 0.95,
      "reasons": [
        "High priority (P1)",
        "Unblocks 3 other issues",
        "No dependencies",
        "Estimated small scope"
      ],
      "claim_command": "br update beads_rust-xyz --status in_progress",
      "unblocks": ["beads_rust-abc", "beads_rust-def"]
    }
  ],
  "project_health": {
    "total": 150,
    "open": 100,
    "in_progress": 20,
    "blocked": 30,
    "ready": 50
  }
}
```

### Scoring Algorithm
- Priority weight: P0=1.0, P1=0.8, P2=0.6, P3=0.4, P4=0.2
- Unblock multiplier: +0.1 per issue unblocked
- Age bonus: older ready issues get slight boost
- Dependency penalty: issues with many deps score lower

### Implementation
```rust
fn calculate_triage_score(issue: \u0026Issue, graph: \u0026DepGraph) -> f64 {
    let priority_weight = match issue.priority {
        0 => 1.0,
        1 => 0.8,
        2 => 0.6,
        3 => 0.4,
        _ => 0.2,
    };
    let unblock_bonus = graph.dependents(\u0026issue.id).len() as f64 * 0.1;
    priority_weight + unblock_bonus
}
```

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] `br --robot-triage` outputs valid JSON
- [ ] Issues are sorted by score descending
- [ ] Reasons explain the ranking
- [ ] claim_command is correct and runnable
- [ ] Project health summary included

Implement field-level validation rules for all entities (issues, dependencies, labels, comments). Validation ensures data integrity and provides clear error messages when constraints are violated.

## Validation Rules from Documentation

### Issue Fields
```rust
pub struct IssueValidator;

impl IssueValidator {
    pub fn validate(issue: &Issue) -> Result<(), Vec<ValidationError>> {
        let mut errors = Vec::new();
        
        // ID: Required, matches prefix pattern, max 50 chars
        if issue.id.is_empty() {
            errors.push(ValidationError::field("id", "cannot be empty"));
        }
        if issue.id.len() > 50 {
            errors.push(ValidationError::field("id", "exceeds 50 characters"));
        }
        if !is_valid_id_format(&issue.id) {
            errors.push(ValidationError::field("id", "invalid format (expected prefix-hash)"));
        }
        
        // Title: Required, max 500 chars
        if issue.title.is_empty() {
            errors.push(ValidationError::field("title", "cannot be empty"));
        }
        if issue.title.len() > 500 {
            errors.push(ValidationError::field("title", "exceeds 500 characters"));
        }
        
        // Description: Optional, max 100KB
        if let Some(ref desc) = issue.description {
            if desc.len() > 102400 {
                errors.push(ValidationError::field("description", "exceeds 100KB"));
            }
        }
        
        // Status: Must be valid enum value
        // (Handled by Status::from_str)
        
        // Priority: 0-4 range
        if issue.priority > 4 {
            errors.push(ValidationError::field("priority", "must be 0-4"));
        }
        
        // IssueType: Must be valid enum value
        // (Handled by IssueType::from_str)
        
        // Timestamps: created_at <= updated_at
        if issue.updated_at < issue.created_at {
            errors.push(ValidationError::field("updated_at", "cannot be before created_at"));
        }
        
        // ExternalRef: Optional, max 200 chars, no spaces
        if let Some(ref ext) = issue.external_ref {
            if ext.len() > 200 {
                errors.push(ValidationError::field("external_ref", "exceeds 200 characters"));
            }
            if ext.contains(char::is_whitespace) {
                errors.push(ValidationError::field("external_ref", "cannot contain whitespace"));
            }
        }
        
        if errors.is_empty() {
            Ok(())
        } else {
            Err(errors)
        }
    }
}

fn is_valid_id_format(id: &str) -> bool {
    // Format: prefix-hash (e.g., "bd-abc123")
    let parts: Vec<&str> = id.splitn(2, "-").collect();
    if parts.len() != 2 {
        return false;
    }
    let prefix = parts[0];
    let hash = parts[1];
    
    // Prefix: 1-10 lowercase alphanumeric
    if prefix.is_empty() || prefix.len() > 10 || !prefix.chars().all(|c| c.is_ascii_lowercase() || c.is_ascii_digit()) {
        return false;
    }
    
    // Hash: 3-8 lowercase alphanumeric
    if hash.len() < 3 || hash.len() > 8 || !hash.chars().all(|c| c.is_ascii_lowercase() || c.is_ascii_digit()) {
        return false;
    }
    
    true
}
```

### Dependency Rules
```rust
pub struct DependencyValidator;

impl DependencyValidator {
    pub fn validate(dep: &Dependency, storage: &SqliteStorage) -> Result<(), Vec<ValidationError>> {
        let mut errors = Vec::new();
        
        // Self-dependency not allowed
        if dep.issue_id == dep.depends_on_id {
            errors.push(ValidationError::field("depends_on_id", "issue cannot depend on itself"));
        }
        
        // Both issues must exist
        if !storage.id_exists(&dep.issue_id)? {
            errors.push(ValidationError::field("issue_id", "issue not found"));
        }
        if !storage.id_exists(&dep.depends_on_id)? {
            errors.push(ValidationError::field("depends_on_id", "dependency target not found"));
        }
        
        // Cycle detection
        if storage.would_create_cycle(&dep.issue_id, &dep.depends_on_id)? {
            errors.push(ValidationError::field("depends_on_id", "would create dependency cycle"));
        }
        
        // Duplicate check
        if storage.dependency_exists(&dep.issue_id, &dep.depends_on_id)? {
            errors.push(ValidationError::field("depends_on_id", "dependency already exists"));
        }
        
        // Dependency type: Must be valid
        // (Handled by DependencyType::from_str)
        
        if errors.is_empty() {
            Ok(())
        } else {
            Err(errors)
        }
    }
}
```

### Label Rules
```rust
pub struct LabelValidator;

impl LabelValidator {
    pub fn validate(label: &str) -> Result<(), ValidationError> {
        // Non-empty
        if label.is_empty() {
            return Err(ValidationError::field("label", "cannot be empty"));
        }
        
        // Max 50 chars
        if label.len() > 50 {
            return Err(ValidationError::field("label", "exceeds 50 characters"));
        }
        
        // Allowed characters: alphanumeric, hyphen, underscore
        if !label.chars().all(|c| c.is_ascii_alphanumeric() || c == "-" || c == "_") {
            return Err(ValidationError::field("label", "invalid characters (only alphanumeric, hyphen, underscore allowed)"));
        }
        
        Ok(())
    }
}
```

### Comment Rules
```rust
pub struct CommentValidator;

impl CommentValidator {
    pub fn validate(comment: &Comment) -> Result<(), Vec<ValidationError>> {
        let mut errors = Vec::new();
        
        // ID: Required
        if comment.id.is_empty() {
            errors.push(ValidationError::field("id", "cannot be empty"));
        }
        
        // IssueID: Required, must exist
        if comment.issue_id.is_empty() {
            errors.push(ValidationError::field("issue_id", "cannot be empty"));
        }
        
        // Content: Required, max 50KB
        if comment.content.is_empty() {
            errors.push(ValidationError::field("content", "cannot be empty"));
        }
        if comment.content.len() > 51200 {
            errors.push(ValidationError::field("content", "exceeds 50KB"));
        }
        
        // Author: Required, max 200 chars
        if comment.author.is_empty() {
            errors.push(ValidationError::field("author", "cannot be empty"));
        }
        if comment.author.len() > 200 {
            errors.push(ValidationError::field("author", "exceeds 200 characters"));
        }
        
        if errors.is_empty() {
            Ok(())
        } else {
            Err(errors)
        }
    }
}
```

### ValidationError Type
```rust
#[derive(Debug, Clone, thiserror::Error)]
pub struct ValidationError {
    pub field: String,
    pub message: String,
}

impl ValidationError {
    pub fn field(field: &str, message: &str) -> Self {
        Self {
            field: field.into(),
            message: message.into(),
        }
    }
}

impl std::fmt::Display for ValidationError {
    fn fmt(&self, f: &mut std::fmt::Formatter) -> std::fmt::Result {
        write!(f, "{}: {}", self.field, self.message)
    }
}
```

The sync command is critical for the SQLite+JSONL hybrid architecture. It must export to JSONL identically and import consistently. This is where most data integrity concerns arise.

 {"id":"beads_rust-min","title":"Create multi-platform installer script","description":"# Multi-Platform Installer Script

## Purpose
Enable one-liner installation of br on Linux, macOS, and Windows with automatic platform detection, checksum verification, and idempotent behavior.

## Technical Requirements

### Script Features
- Platform detection (Linux/macOS/Windows)
- Architecture detection (x86_64/arm64)
- Download from GitHub Releases
- SHA256 checksum verification
- Fallback to source build if binary unavailable
- Idempotent (safe to re-run)
- Lock mechanism for concurrent protection
- Resume capability for interrupted downloads
- PATH modification with shell detection
- Proxy support (HTTPS_PROXY)

### Installation Methods
```bash
# Primary: curl
curl -fsSL https://raw.githubusercontent.com/Dicklesworthstone/beads_rust/main/install.sh | bash

# Alternative: wget
wget -qO- https://raw.githubusercontent.com/Dicklesworthstone/beads_rust/main/install.sh | bash

# With options
curl -fsSL .../install.sh | bash -s -- --prefix=~/.local --no-modify-path
```

### Script Structure
```bash
#\!/usr/bin/env bash
set -euo pipefail

# Configuration
REPO="Dicklesworthstone/beads_rust"
BINARY_NAME="br"
INSTALL_DIR="${HOME}/.local/bin"

# Functions
detect_platform()     # linux_amd64, darwin_arm64, etc.
detect_shell()        # bash, zsh, fish
download_release()    # curl with retry
verify_checksum()     # sha256sum verification
install_binary()      # atomic install
modify_path()         # idempotent PATH update
build_from_source()   # fallback with Rust install

# Main
main() {
    parse_args "$@"
    acquire_lock
    trap cleanup EXIT
    
    PLATFORM="$(detect_platform)"
    
    if \! download_release "$PLATFORM"; then
        warn "Binary not available, building from source..."
        build_from_source
    fi
    
    verify_checksum
    install_binary
    [[ "$MODIFY_PATH" == "true" ]] \u0026\u0026 modify_path
    
    echo "✓ br installed successfully\!"
    echo "  Run 'br --help' to get started."
}
```

### Checksum Verification
```bash
verify_checksum() {
    local expected="$(curl -fsSL "${RELEASE_URL}.sha256")"
    local actual="$(sha256sum "$DOWNLOAD_PATH" | cut -d' ' -f1)"
    
    if [[ "$expected" \!= "$actual" ]]; then
        error "Checksum mismatch\! Expected: $expected, Got: $actual"
        exit 1
    fi
}
```

### Idempotency Patterns
- mkdir -p (creates if not exists)
- Check before PATH modify (grep for existing entry)
- Lock file with stale detection
- Atomic moves (mv, not cp)

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Works on Ubuntu 22.04+, macOS 13+, Windows 11 (WSL)
- [ ] Detects x86_64 and arm64 architectures
- [ ] Verifies checksums before install
- [ ] Falls back to source build gracefully
- [ ] Idempotent (multiple runs don't break)
- [ ] Modifies PATH correctly for bash/zsh/fish
- [ ] Works behind HTTPS_PROXY
- [ ] Clear error messages on failure

## Files to Create
- `install.sh` - Main installer script
- `scripts/build-release.sh` - Build script for releases
- `.github/workflows/release.yml` - Release automation

## References
- ACFS install.sh patterns
- self_update crate documentation
- Homebrew installer conventions","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"StormyBarn","estimated_minutes":0,"created_at":"2026-01-16T18:50:10.392094598Z","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","updated_at":"2026-01-17T03:52:49.473288374Z","closed_at":"2026-01-17T03:52:49.473191582Z"}

 {"id":"beads_rust-uin","title":"prime Command Implementation","description":"## Overview
Implement the `br prime` command for displaying context about the current beads state. This is designed for AI agents to quickly understand the project's issue state after context loss (e.g., after compaction or starting a new session).

## CLI Interface
```
br prime [OPTIONS]

Options:
  --full                      Include all context (ready + in_progress + recent)
  --ready-only                Only show ready issues
  --limit <N>                 Limit issues per category (default: 10)
  --json                      Output as JSON
  --robot                     Machine-readable output
```

## Output Structure

The prime command outputs a comprehensive snapshot for agent context recovery:

```
# Beads Context Recovery
═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════

## Project: beads_rust
Prefix: beads_rust-
Schema: v3
Issues: 156 total (42 open, 8 in_progress, 106 closed)

## In Progress (8)
Your active work:

[P0] beads_rust-abc123  Fix critical auth bug
     Assignee: alice | Updated: 2 hours ago
     Blocked by: (none - ready to work)

[P1] beads_rust-def456  Implement user dashboard
     Assignee: bob | Updated: 1 day ago
     Blocked by: beads_rust-xyz789 (Config system)

## Ready to Work (top 10)
Unblocked issues by priority:

[P0] beads_rust-ghi789  Security patch for XSS
[P1] beads_rust-jkl012  Add pagination to list API
[P1] beads_rust-mno345  Refactor error handling
...

## Recently Updated (top 5)
Latest activity:

[closed] beads_rust-pqr678  Setup CI/CD pipeline
         Closed 3 hours ago by alice

[updated] beads_rust-stu901  Database migration scripts
          Updated 5 hours ago (status: open → in_progress)

## Critical Blockers
Issues blocking the most work:

1. beads_rust-xyz789 (Config system) - blocks 5 issues
2. beads_rust-vwx234 (Auth middleware) - blocks 3 issues

───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Commands: br ready | br show <id> | br update <id> --status=in_progress
```

### Implementation
```rust
fn prime(&self, opts: PrimeOptions) -> Result<PrimeContext> {
    let stats = self.get_stats()?;
    let in_progress = self.list_issues(ListQuery { 
        status: Some(Status::InProgress),
        limit: opts.limit,
        ..Default::default()
    })?;
    let ready = self.get_ready_issues(ReadyFilters {
        limit: opts.limit,
        ..Default::default()
    })?;
    let recent = self.get_recent_activity(opts.limit)?;
    let blockers = self.find_critical_blockers(5)?;
    
    Ok(PrimeContext {
        stats,
        in_progress,
        ready,
        recent,
        blockers,
    })
}
```

## JSON Output
```json
{
  "project": "beads_rust",
  "stats": {
    "total": 156,
    "open": 42,
    "in_progress": 8,
    "closed": 106
  },
  "in_progress": [...],
  "ready": [...],
  "recent": [...],
  "critical_blockers": [...]
}
```

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Show project summary (name, prefix, counts)
- [ ] List in-progress issues
- [ ] List ready-to-work issues
- [ ] Show recent activity
- [ ] Identify critical blockers
- [ ] Human-readable and JSON output
- [ ] Configurable limits
- [ ] Fast execution (< 100ms)

## Dependencies
- Requires stats Command infrastructure
- Requires ready Command infrastructure
- Requires blocked Command infrastructure

## Rationale
AI agents lose context frequently due to context window limits. The prime command provides everything an agent needs to resume work effectively. It answers: "What was I working on? What can I work on? What's blocking progress?"
